title: The Oak
content:
Live thy Life,
Young and old,
Like yon oak,
Bright in spring,
Living gold;

Summer-rich
Then; and then
Autumn-changed
Soberer-hued
Gold again.

All his leaves
Fall'n at length,
Look, he stands,
Trunk and bough
Naked strength.
meaning:
Alfred Lord Tennyson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Oak"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
